title: pergularia hamiltonii
intro: Pergularia hamiltonii, also known as Satavar or Chanda, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly found in India and other parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and aiding in digestion. Pergularia hamiltonii can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or powder, which can be consumed orally or used topically to treat various health conditions.
